definition: SFAS 142 goodwill impairment testing creates a reflexive channel for monetary policy: lower policy rates mechanically raise fair values in goodwill testing, preventing recognition of underlying cash flow deterioration. Higher rates trigger impairments ($80B+ surge in 2022-23) that appear as non-GAAP adjustments, masking transmission.
evidence: $80B+ impairment surge in 2022-23 directly traceable to rate increases
Goodwill impairments nearly tripled from $3 to $8/share in S&P 500
Lower rates mechanically raise fair values in SFAS 142 testing
Mechanism is reflexive — rate cuts would hide impairment, rate hikes reveal it
open_questions: How much of the non-GAAP adjustment expansion is specifically SFAS 142 driven vs other categories?
Does the mechanism create a false signal of recovery when rates decline?
